newsSinceJun6: ADR whipsaw: TSM +3.8% Jun 8 on AI demand/2nm yield momentum, then -4.1% Jun 9 after bipartisan US senators urged stricter export rules to block advanced chips reaching Chinese subsidiaries abroad; Taiwan shares only +0.44% today. AGM (Jun 4) backdrop still echoing: CEO C.C. Wei said it will be 'a long time' before TSMC can meet customer demand but pledged stable pricing (no hikes); 2026 revenue guided above +30% USD. May monthly revenue due ~Jun 12.

sources.label: TradingKey - TSM -4.10% on Jun 9 (senator export-rule push)
TradingKey - TSM +3.78% on Jun 8 (AI demand, 2nm yields)
Tom's Hardware - Wei: long time before we can meet customer demand; stable pricing
TSMC investor relations - financial calendar (May revenue ~Jun 12)

verdicts.rankRationale: Rank 3 still fits the silicon-basket bottleneck role: Wei's undersupply reiteration confirms the manufacturing-chokepoint thesis, but the new US senator export-rule push and a no-price-hike pledge add regulatory noise and cap margin upside on a crowded ~$1.94T mega-cap with no fresh residual alpha.
verdicts.bull: Structurally sold out at the leading edge with the CEO saying demand cannot be met 'for a long time'; 2026 revenue guided above +30% USD with 2nm ramping at strong yields - the purest manufacturing bottleneck in the silicon basket.
verdicts.neutral: Local shares drifted +0.44% while the ADR whipsawed on Washington headlines; no new fundamentals since the AGM, and the May revenue print on ~Jun 12 is the next real data point.
verdicts.bear: Bipartisan export-control tightening on chips reaching Chinese subsidiaries could clip a revenue tail; +45% YTD on a $1.94T mega-cap means the bottleneck is fully consensus, and stable-pricing pledges forgo the pricing-power upside the thesis implies.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: May 2026 monthly revenue release (~Jun 12) confirming the >30% USD growth trajectory.
verdicts.primaryRisk: US export-control escalation (senator push on Chinese subsidiaries abroad) compressing the China-exposed revenue tail and multiple.
